Case title: Study-type classification and review triage for social-policy evidence synthesis
Short description: Benchmark specialized classifiers and LLMs for study-type classification in reviews where social-policy evidence mixes methods and weak metadata.
Empirical unit of analysis: Abstracts and study records from review corpora
Key typology question: When does predictive triage work well enough that human corpus-boundary work, not manual triage, becomes decisive?
Observable artifacts / traces: Study-type labels, classifier probabilities, human override logs
Key outcomes observed: Sensitivity/specificity by source type, triage speed, error concentration
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: Predictive triage works equally well on heterogeneous and poorly structured sources
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Benchmark corpora may be cleaner than actual policy-review inputs
Why this case has high leverage: It isolates the triage function from broader synthesis claims.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
